content: We can refactor curiosity-driven autonomous agent intrinsic reward mechanisms to directly address the 2024-2025 SOTA active learning agent memory scaling bottleneck by repurposing ICM (Intrinsic Curiosity Module) prediction error as a dynamic memory pruning signal that only retains information the agent cannot yet explain or compress, rather than pruning by recency or usage frequency. This creates a self-organizing memory that prioritizes high-uncertainty, learning-relevant data aligned with the agent's active exploration goals.
